skill.name: equipment-maintenance-log
skill.description: Track lab equipment calibration dates and send maintenance reminders for pipettes, balances, centrifuges, and other instruments. Validates date formats and supports update/delete operations.
skill.summary: Track and manage lab equipment calibration schedules with reminders and compliance reports.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer found 72 potential issues across 4 files (740 lines). Evaluation confirms all are false positives except expected filesystem access for data persistence. The weak cryptographic algorithm findings (10 instances) stem from date parsing function names (strptime) being misidentified. System reconnaissance findings (14 instances) and shell backtick findings (34 instances) are from scanning documentation and audit result files, not actual code execution. Network findings (2 instances) reference a documentation URL only. The only legitimate finding is local file storage at ~/.openclaw/equipment_log.json, which is expected behavior. No credential access, network requests, or malicious patterns were confirmed.

low_findings.title: Hidden file data storage in home directory
low_findings.description: Script stores equipment data in ~/.openclaw/equipment_log.json in the user's home directory. This is expected behavior for a CLI persistence tool, but the hidden directory path should be documented for user awareness.

low_findings.title: Local file write for data persistence
low_findings.description: Script writes equipment records to a JSON file on the local filesystem. This is required for the skill's data persistence functionality and does not pose a security risk beyond expected local file access.

content.user_title: Track Lab Equipment Calibration and Maintenance
content.value_statement: Lab equipment calibration tracking requires careful record-keeping to maintain compliance. This skill manages calibration schedules, generates compliance reports, and sends maintenance reminders for pipettes, balances, centrifuges, and other instruments.
actual_capabilities: Add equipment records with name, calibration date, and interval in months
Update calibration dates after equipment servicing
Delete decommissioned equipment from the log
Check for upcoming and overdue maintenance with 30, 60, and 90-day alerts
Generate JSON compliance reports with equipment status summaries
Validate date format before storing data
limitations: Stores data only on the local machine with no sharing between users or devices
Does not send email or push notifications for maintenance reminders
Cannot import or export data in formats other than JSON
Time calculations use 30-day months, not calendar months

use_cases.title: Lab Manager Compliance Audit
use_cases.description: Generate a compliance report showing all equipment calibration status, overdue items, and upcoming due dates for regulatory review.

use_cases.title: Research Technician Maintenance Check
use_cases.description: Check which lab equipment needs calibration this week and get a list of overdue instruments.

use_cases.title: Facilities Coordinator Equipment Logging
use_cases.description: Add newly acquired instruments to the calibration tracking system with their initial calibration dates and intervals.

prompt_templates.title: Add New Equipment
prompt_templates.prompt: Add pipette P100 to the maintenance log with calibration date 2024-01-15 and 12-month interval.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user adding a piece of equipment to begin tracking

prompt_templates.title: Check Maintenance Status
prompt_templates.prompt: Check all equipment maintenance status and tell me which items are overdue or due within 30 days.
prompt_templates.scenario: Regular maintenance review to identify upcoming tasks

prompt_templates.title: Generate Compliance Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a compliance report for our lab audit showing all equipment status.
prompt_templates.scenario: Preparing documentation for regulatory inspection or audit

prompt_templates.title: Update and Verify After Servicing
prompt_templates.prompt: Update pipette P100 calibration date to 2025-01-15 after servicing, then show me the updated compliance report.

output_examples.input: Check maintenance status for all equipment.
Generate a compliance report for our lab audit showing all equipment status.
output: A formatted JSON compliance report containing:
- Report date, total equipment count, and summary section
- Per-equipment records with name, location, last calibration date,
interval months, next due date, days until due, and status
- Status values: OVERDUE, DUE_SOON, or OK
- Summary block with counts for overdue, due within 30 days, and OK

output_examples.input: Add centrifuge Z400 with calibration date 2024-06-01 and 6-month interval in Lab 2A.
output: The equipment was added successfully with:

best_practices: Always include --calibration-date in YYYY-MM-DD format when adding equipment to ensure proper date validation.
Run a maintenance check after adding or updating equipment to verify the schedule is correct.
Use the --report option before audits to generate a complete compliance summary for inspectors.
anti_patterns: Do not omit --interval when adding equipment because the calibration interval in months is required.
Do not use date formats other than YYYY-MM-DD because the script validates and rejects other formats.
Do not assume the tool sends email or push notifications because it only displays alerts in the terminal.

faq.question: What types of equipment can I track?
faq.answer: Any lab instrument that requires regular calibration, such as pipettes, balances, centrifuges, pH meters, and similar equipment.

faq.question: Where is my equipment data stored?
faq.answer: Data is stored in a local JSON file at ~/.openclaw/equipment_log.json on your machine.

faq.question: Can multiple users share the same equipment log?
faq.answer: No. The log is stored locally on each machine. There is no multi-user or network sharing capability.

faq.question: What happens if I enter an invalid date?
faq.answer: The script rejects invalid dates such as 2024-13-45 with a clear error message and does not store any data.

faq.question: Can I update or delete equipment records?
faq.answer: Yes. Use --update to change calibration dates and --delete to remove decommissioned equipment.

faq.question: How are maintenance intervals calculated?
faq.answer: Each equipment record stores a calibration interval in months. The next due date is the last calibration date plus the interval using 30-day months.
